decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with cards like Golgari Grave-Troll, Golgari Thug, and Stinkweed Imp in order to trigger Dredge and bring back strong creatures like Ichorid and Prized Amalgam. The deck utilizes cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Noxious Revival to facilitate graveyard manipulation and recursion. It also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect the combo. By efficiently dredging and utilizing graveyard synergies, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a powerful graveyard-based strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Stock Up to generate card advantage and mana, allowing you to combo off with cards like Tolarian Academy, Lotus Petal, and Sol Ring to generate a large storm count. This allows you to win the game through either casting Brain Freeze or Flame of Anor to mill your opponent's library or deal direct damage to their life total. The deck also makes use of powerful restricted cards like Ancestral Recall and Time Walk to further enhance your combo potential.

The deck focuses on assembling powerful artifact combos quickly, using cards like Transmute Artifact, Tinker, and Paradox Engine to tutor for key pieces and generate infinite mana and card advantage. The goal is to ramp into powerful cards like Coveted Jewel and Karn, the Great Creator, locking opponents out with cards like Void Mirror and Stock Up while generating value with cards like Oboro, Palace in the Clouds and Phyrexian Metamorph. By utilizing fast mana sources like Moxen and Mishra's Workshop, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a powerful artifact-based strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game using a combination of counterspells, card draw, and disruptive spells. The inclusion of Gifts Ungiven allows the player to search their library for specific cards to add to their hand, while key cards like Ancestral Recall, Time Walk, and Demonic Tutor provide powerful plays. The deck also features a variety of mana acceleration cards like the Moxen and Sol Ring to help cast spells quickly. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and gain an advantage through card advantage and powerful spells.

This deck focuses on using disruption and removal spells to control the game, while utilizing powerful draw spells and tutoring effects to maintain card advantage and find key pieces. The deck also includes a companion that provides additional value and synergy with the deck's strategy. Key cards include Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Lavinia, Azorius Renegade to disrupt opponents, along with card draw spells like Dig Through Time and Ancestral Recall to keep the resources flowing. Additionally, the deck utilizes mana denial with Wasteland and Strip Mine to slow down opponents' mana development.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy by using hate cards like Archon of Emeria and Void Mirror while applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Clarion Conqueror and White Plume Adventurer. It also has powerful mana acceleration from cards like Moxen and Ancient Tomb to quickly deploy threats. The deck further hinders the opponent's game plan through land destruction with Wasteland and Strip Mine. Overall, the deck looks to control the game through disruptive elements and efficient beatdown creatures.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Combo - Paradoxical Outcome deck focuses on generating card advantage and mana with powerful artifacts like Black Lotus, Sol Ring, and Moxen. By casting Paradoxical Outcome, the deck can return multiple artifacts to hand, draw cards, and generate mana to combo off with cards like Tolarian Academy and Time Walk. The deck also includes disruption spells like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares to keep the opponent in check while building up its own resources for a powerful combo finish.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Paradoxical Outcome card to generate massive card advantage and mana advantage by bouncing a high number of artifacts back to hand. The goal is to draw into key combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and abilities. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares to control the board and protect the combo. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds additional versatility and recursion options to the deck.

This deck is built around using Paradoxical Outcome to draw a large number of cards and generate mana to go off with a powerful combo. It includes various fast mana sources like Moxen and Sol Ring, along with Tolarian Academy to generate additional mana. The deck also includes disruption in the form of counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm, as well as removal like Swords to Plowshares. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and win the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

The key strategy of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan with counterspells and removal, while generating card advantage through cards like Ancestral Recall and Ponder. The deck also utilizes cards like Urza's Saga and Lórien Revealed to create incremental advantages. Additionally, the inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for extra value by returning key cards from the graveyard. Overall, the deck aims to control the game and outmaneuver the opponent through resource management and efficient threats.

This Vintage Aggro-MUD deck focuses on assembling powerful artifacts and locking down the opponent's resources to create a strong board presence and ultimately overwhelm the opponent with efficient and explosive plays. By utilizing cards like Mishra's Workshop, Grim Monolith, and The One Ring, this deck aims to generate massive mana advantage and quickly deploy threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to put pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void to limit the opponent's options and secure victory.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Combo - Coveted Jewel deck focuses on quickly assembling powerful artifacts and using them to generate immense mana and draw cards. By combining cards like Tinker, Paradox Engine, and Coveted Jewel, the deck aims to create explosive turns that overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of powerful counterspells like Force of Will helps protect the combo pieces, while cards like Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb provide additional mana acceleration. Ultimately, the goal is to outpace the opponent and win the game through a powerful artifact-based combo.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of Coveted Jewel and Paradoxical Outcome to generate mana and draw cards, allowing for explosive plays and the ability to win the game in a single turn. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Time Vault and The One Ring, along with disruptive spells like Force of Will, allows the deck to protect its combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With cards like Tinker and Karn, the Great Creator to tutor for key pieces, the deck aims to control the game and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful synergies.

This deck focuses on using efficient counterspells like Force of Will and Mana Drain to control the game while leveraging powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Gifts Ungiven. It also utilizes graveyard recursion with cards like Underworld Breach and Urza's Saga to create explosive plays. The inclusion of versatile cards like Brainstorm and Demonic Tutor allow for flexibility and adaptability in different situations. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents through careful resource management and strategic decision-making.

This deck focuses on utilizing control elements such as counterspells, removal, and disruption to slow down the opponent's game plan and take control of the game. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional recursion and value over the course of the game. The deck also features powerful card draw and selection with cards like Ancestral Recall, Brainstorm, and Dig Through Time. The mana base is well-rounded with fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on utilizing powerful control spells and disruptive creatures to outmaneuver opponents. With a heavy emphasis on counter magic and removal, along with card advantage and graveyard manipulation, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while digging for key cards to close out the game. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value and recursion options. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through disruption, resource denial, and card advantage to secure victories in Vintage 1 vs 1 matches.
